edits.slides.7.content: The touch context set the floor for every interaction pattern. Hover states, right-click menus, and precise pointer interactions were ruled out — tap targets had to work for gloved hands, and every interaction had to be achievable by tap alone as a baseline.
edits.slides.15.title: Scan
edits.slides.15.subtitle: Capturing a 3D model before lab submission — starting with where the tools should live.
edits.slides.17.title: Where should the tools live?
edits.slides.17.dir1Desc: Bottom center — sat across the occlusal area, obscuring the scan detail that needed the most visual attention during capture.
edits.slides.17.dir2Desc: Vertical right rail — consistent location, but always-visible consumed viewport space even when no tool was needed.
edits.slides.17.dir3Desc: Top-right corner, collapsible — outside the active scan area, reachable without disrupting capture. Fixing placement exposed the icon problem: a toolbar in the right place with unmemorable icons was still a broken toolbar.
edits.slides.19.title: Managing multiple scans and comparing them
edits.slides.19.content: Pre-treatment and post-treatment scans, organised by tab, with real-time overlay and adjustable opacity.
edits.slides.19.highlight: Comparing two scans became visual instead of mental.

slides.7.issues: Three surfaces — scanner hardware (Element and Lumina), MIDC desktop, and myitero.com — one design across all three
Backward-compatible with in-progress cases still on the old Rx
Feature flag (SWO NewFlowRx) — no persistent onboarding; learnable on first contact, every time
Read mode and demo mode designed in from the start, not retrofitted
M&I, Labs, and iTero Labs out of MVP scope — but the IA was built to absorb them
slides.19.bullets2: Add scans within one session — tabs keep them organised
Overlay scans with adjustable opacity — compare in real time
What clinicians held in their head, they could now see directly

slide.title: View
slide.subtitle: Validating the 3D model before lab submission — and giving clinicians a clear sense of done.

ops.reason: Restore View chapter — designer wants the View arc back

slide.label: View — Before
slide.title: No signal that the work was done
slide.content: Unlabelled icons. No hierarchy between primary and destructive actions. No completion signal. Clinicians re-checked every scan by habit — not because anything was wrong, but because nothing told them they were done.
slide.highlight: Re-checking is a trust symptom, not a workflow preference.
